  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of Triple objects describing an artist (or related entity)
      into a single fluent English sentence.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # Use the subject of the first triple as the main entity
      main_subject = triples[0].subject
  
      # Build a readable phrase for each predicate‑object pair
      phrases = []
      for t in triples:
          # Make predicate more readable (replace underscores, keep original wording)
          pred = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
          # Simple handling for common patterns
          if pred.lower().endswith("date") or pred.lower().endswith("year"):
              phrase = f"{pred} {t.object}"
          else:
              phrase = f"{pred} {t.object}"
          phrases.append(phrase)
  
      # Join the phrases into a natural‑language list
      if len(phrases) == 1:
          body = phrases[0]
      else:
          body = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]

with:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of Triple objects describing an artist (or related entity)
      into a single fluent English sentence.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # Main entity is the subject of the first triple
      main_subject = triples[0].subject
  
      # Helper to turn a predicate/object into a natural clause
      def clause(t: Triple) -> str:
          obj = t.object.strip('"')
          p = t.predicate
          # mapping of predicates to natural language patterns
          if p in ("associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist",):
              return f"is a member of {obj}"
          if p == "background":
              return f"is a {obj}"
          if p == "genre":
              return f"performs {obj.lower()} music"
          if p == "instrument":
              return f"plays the {obj}"
          if p == "origin":
              return f"originates from {obj}"
          if p == "birthDate":
              return f"was born on {obj}"
          if p == "birthPlace":
              return f"was born in {obj}"
  ... (38 more lines)
